action: Create a machine-enforced trigger receipt chain that prevents unsupported promotion from request or scheduler acceptance to peer completion.
selected_because: Continuity mining identified the absence of a shared correlation schema linking requester, trigger acknowledgement, peer run identity, output commit, and verification. Existing R-002 trace correlation and M-004 language separation did not enforce state transitions.
finding: Each coordination stage is a separate occurrence. Correlation metadata connects records but does not prove later stages occurred.
acceptance_contract: each receipt cryptographically links to its predecessor
completion is asserted only at peer_completion_verified
verification: Source and six deterministic test cases are committed. Runtime execution was unavailable, so test passage is not claimed.
handoff: Continuity Mining should adopt the five-state vocabulary; all teams should emit receipts using one correlation_id; a later frontier run should add JSON Schema, signatures, replay protection, and independent evidence resolvers after the Node tests pass.
